rationale: Covers foundational DNA/RNA concepts, genome sequencing applications, and specific gene-editing tools like CRISPR-Cas9, ZFNs, and TALENs.
Focuses on the status of GM crops in India (Bt Cotton, Brinjal, Mustard) and the regulatory roles of GEAC, RCGM, and FSSAI.
Details India's first genome-edited rice varieties and the objectives of the GenomeIndia Project.
Groups emerging technologies like Organ-on-Chip and RNA silencing with forensic tools like DNA profiling and the SHUKR gene.

subtopic: Specialized Medical Therapies and Stem Cells
rationale: Combines advanced cellular treatments like CAR-T therapy and Mitochondrial Replacement Therapy with stem cell classifications.
Links the BioE3 policy and National Biobank initiative with the DEEP project's focus on epigenetic epidemiology.
Covers AI stages, machine learning types, and India's sovereign AI initiatives like Bharat Gen and the IndiaAI Mission.
Addresses technical risks like Deepfakes (GANs) alongside specialized hardware (TPU) and economic paradoxes of technology.
Explains core quantum principles (superposition, entanglement) and the 2025 Nobel Prize for macroscopic quantum tunnelling.
Details the National Quantum Mission, QKD protocols, and advanced concepts like quantum scrambling and security chips (QSIP).
Groups human-machine interfaces (Neuralink), additive manufacturing, and the regulatory landscape for stablecoins and crypto assets.
Covers connectivity (Bluetooth Mesh), physical infrastructure (Undersea cables, RFID), and industrial applications like Digital Twins and robotic surgery.

subtopic: Black Holes, Gravitational Waves and Neutrinos
rationale: Focuses on large-scale astrophysical phenomena and the global network of observatories like LIGO and JUNO.
Covers CERN's LHC, the Higgs Boson, matter-antimatter asymmetry, and the role of Fast Radio Bursts in measuring cosmic matter.
Details the NISAR mission, Gaganyaan's human spaceflight goals, and the Aditya-L1 solar mission.
Focuses on ISRO's SpaDeX docking technology, Chandrayaan-3 findings, and the private Axiom-4 mission to the ISS.
Covers the rise of private EO constellations (Pixxel) and the technical characteristics of different satellite orbits (LEO, MEO, GEO, SSO).
Lists international missions (Lucy, Solar Orbiter) and compares cryogenic vs. semi-cryogenic engine technologies.
Details India's launch vehicle capabilities (PSLV, GSLV, LMLV), analog missions in Ladakh, and global satellite internet projects.
Addresses space hazards like CMEs and debris alongside astronomical discoveries like Brown Dwarfs and Super Earths.

subtopic: Private Space Sector and Solar Observatories
rationale: Focuses on the Vikram-1 private launcher and historical solar data from the Kodaikanal Observatory.
Comprehensive coverage of TB (MDR/XDR), HIV prevention, Hepatitis strains, and the Mpox outbreak.
Links the 2025 Nobel Prize in Medicine (immune tolerance) with the global threat of AMR and the regulation of FDC drugs.
Explains various vaccine platforms (Live, Inactivated, Recombinant) and breakthroughs in oncolytic and mRNA cancer vaccines.
Details the Universal Immunization Programme, One Health mission, and the global effort to eliminate industrially produced trans fats.
Covers the newly recognized Type 5 diabetes and advanced cancer treatment using Proton Arc Therapy (SPArc).
Details India's layered air defence architecture, including S-400, Barak 8, Akash, and the Akashteer control system.
Compares cruise and ballistic missiles and details the Agni series (including MIRV capability) and the historical IGMDP.
Focuses on supersonic/hypersonic missiles like BrahMos and Astra, and the mechanics of Ramjet vs. Scramjet engines.
Groups dedicated military satellites (GSAT-7 series) with laser-directed energy weapons and HAPS airships.
Covers 5th gen fighters (AMCA), naval jets (Rafale M), indigenous UAVs (Bhargavastra), and the Netra MkII AWACS.
Compares fission and fusion processes and details India's Tokamak research and the Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR).
Explains India's three-stage nuclear power programme and the strategic importance of the thorium fuel cycle.
Focuses on alternatives to Lithium-ion, specifically Sodium-Ion and Vanadium Redox Flow batteries.
Links geospatial tools (GIS/GPS) and Operation Dronagiri with the strategic Quad Critical Minerals Initiative.
Covers the distribution of REEs in India and the application of nanotechnology in medicine (Nanozymes) and agriculture (Nano fertilizers).
Groups Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOFs) with electric vehicle indices, V2G technology, and Hyperloop systems.

subtopic: Deep Ocean Mission and RDI Scheme
rationale: Details the Samudrayaan mission (Matsya-6000) and the Research Development and Innovation (RDI) financing scheme.
Covers atmospheric sensors (SODAR), railway safety (Kavach), and new magnetic materials like Altermagnets.
Focuses on Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) and the regulation of plant biostimulants.
